mechanism_narrative: Persistently near-zero FEDFUNDS keeps the equity risk premium compressed relative to bond yields, sustaining a continuous flow of capital into equities. A monotonic multi-month plateau near zero produces a steady positive drift in S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.16% as of 2014-06, near zero lower bound since 2008
failure_condition: Rank IC between FEDFUNDS level and forward 20d returns falls below 0.03 over any rolling 2-year window, or returns during near-zero rate periods are not significantly higher than full-sample average (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When UNRATE declines by more than 0.5 percentage points over a trailing 6-month window, it signals a self-reinforcing labor market recovery that boosts consumer confidence and corporate earnings expectations, driving S&P 500 gains over the subsequent quarter.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE fell from 7.5% to 7.2% over prior months, continuing multi-year decline
failure_condition: In periods where the UNRATE decline trigger fires, 60-day forward returns are positive in less than 55% of instances, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising RSAFS combined with FEDFUNDS held near zero indicates strong consumer demand financed under a supportive monetary backdrop, jointly signaling sustainable earnings growth without offsetting rate-driven valuation compression, producing positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days. Neither series alone captures this synergy: rising retail sales amid rate hikes would instead compress multiples.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 343242 million with FEDFUNDS at 0.16% as of mid-2014
failure_condition: Joint-trigger instances show forward 20d returns statistically indistinguishable from unconditional baseline (t-test p > 0.10), or Rank IC < 0.03 across two consecutive 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: During an industrial recovery regime following a recession, sustained month-over-month gains in INDPRO signal expanding manufacturing capacity utilization that precedes broader corporate profit growth, lifting S&P 500 returns over a quarterly horizon. This effect is strongest only in early-to-mid recovery, not in late-cycle expansions where capacity is already saturated.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 106.1173 (2017=100 base) as of 2014-05, rising steadily post-2009 trough
failure_condition: During the identified recovery regime, INDPRO momentum shows Rank IC < 0.03 with 60-day forward returns, or the relationship reverses sign in more than one 3-year sub-period.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent month-over-month gains in PAYEMS reflect ongoing labor demand strength that markets incorporate quickly, producing a short-lived positive drift in S&P 500 returns over the following trading week as payroll data confirms macro stability.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 135489 thousand persons as of 2014-06, on a multi-year uptrend
failure_condition: 5-day forward return correlation with monthly PAYEMS surprise direction has Rank IC < 0.02 over any rolling 1-year window.

mechanism_narrative: When HOUST housing starts cross above a 600-thousand-unit threshold and continue rising, it signals renewed housing-sector demand that supports broader consumer spending and construction-linked equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST > 600 thousand units and rising for 2 consecutive months
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 625.0 thousand units as of 2014-05, above the 600k threshold
failure_condition: In months where HOUST trigger is active, forward 20d returns are positive in fewer than 55% of instances, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising CPIAUCSL inflation combined with FEDFUNDS still pinned near zero creates an increasing risk of an abrupt policy normalization shock; when inflation accelerates while rates remain suppressed, markets begin pricing forthcoming tightening, generating negative S&P 500 returns over a quarterly horizon as the gap between inflation and policy rate widens beyond historical norms.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 213.06 with FEDFUNDS at 0.16% as of mid-2014, QE taper underway
failure_condition: Joint-trigger instances produce 60-day forward returns not significantly negative (p > 0.10) relative to baseline, or Rank IC magnitude < 0.03 over two consecutive 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: In a low-real-GDP-growth regime where GDPC1 quarterly growth remains below its long-run trend, even modest strengthening in RSAFS retail sales prompts an outsized short-term positive market reaction over the next trading week, as investors interpret consumer resilience as a sign the recovery is broadening despite subdued official growth prints.
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 11712.4 billion chained dollars as of 2014Q1, reflecting sluggish post-recession growth
failure_condition: During below-trend GDP regimes, RSAFS upticks fail to produce 5-day forward returns exceeding baseline by a statistically significant margin (p > 0.10), or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 1-year rolling window.
